facts.claim: You can't connect the dots looking forward; you can only connect them looking backwards.
You have to trust that the dots will somehow connect in your future, trusting in something like your gut, destiny, life, or karma.
You've got to find what you love to do, both for work and for personal relationships.
The only way to be truly satisfied is to do what you believe is great work, and the only way to do great work is to love what you do.
Remembering that you'll be dead soon is the most important tool to help make big choices in life.
In the face of death, almost everything—external expectations, pride, fear of embarrassment or failure—falls away, leaving only what is truly important.
Your time is limited, so don't waste it living someone else's life.
Have the courage to follow your heart and intuition, as they somehow already know what you truly want to become.
'Stay hungry, stay foolish' is a farewell message that the speaker wishes for himself and the graduates.
Believing that the dots will connect down the road gives you the confidence to follow your heart, even when it leads you off the well-worn path.
Getting fired from Apple, though devastating, turned out to be the best thing that could have ever happened, freeing the speaker to enter a creative period.
If you haven't found what you love yet, keep looking, and don't settle.
Don't lose faith when life hits you hard, as the only thing that kept the speaker going after being fired was his love for what he did.
Remembering that you are going to die is the best way to avoid the trap of thinking you have something to lose.
Don't be trapped by dogma, which is living with the results of other people's thinking.
Don't let the noise of others' opinions drown out your own inner voice.
Everything else is secondary to following your heart and intuition.
Death is very likely the single best invention of life, acting as life's change agent by clearing out the old to make way for the new.
Dropping out of Reed College and taking calligraphy classes, which seemed impractical, later proved crucial for designing the Macintosh's typography.
The speaker decided to drop out of an expensive college after six months because he couldn't see the value, had no idea what to do, and was spending his parents' savings.
The Macintosh was the first computer with beautiful typography because of the speaker's calligraphy knowledge.
If the speaker hadn't dropped out and taken the calligraphy class, personal computers might not have the wonderful typography they do.
After being fired from Apple, the speaker started NeXT and Pixar, with Pixar creating 'Toy Story' and becoming the most successful animation studio.
Apple bought NeXT, and the speaker returned to Apple, with NeXT's technology being central to Apple's current renaissance.
The speaker was diagnosed with a rare, curable form of pancreatic cancer and had surgery, and is now fine.

The speaker has looked in the mirror every morning for 33 years, asking 'If today were the last day of my life, would I want to do what I am about to do today?' and changes things if the answer is 'no' too often.
